title: Everyone does as they are told to do — shitty philosophy, don't misinterpret
html:
TL;DR

  • One-line October 2019 aphorism: Everyone does as they are told to do — shitty philosophy — don't misinterpret though.
  • Earliest extant articulation of the conformity-as-default observation that becomes be your own original in later notes — the 17-year-old version is more terse and self-correcting.
  • The hedge — don't misinterpret though — is teen-Yahir doing the asking the right question move on his own aphorism: I might be oversimplifying; treat the line as a starting frame, not a verdict.
  • Sits in an October 2019 cluster with Bill Gates books cope and don't do it for money — the month is a tight self-correction triptych.
  • Functionally a proto-manifesto bullet — the kind of line that, three years later, becomes a sentence in a full manifesto.

Notes

The note is a sentence and a self-edit. "Everyone does as they are told to do" is the observation; "shitty philosophy" is the verdict; "don't misinterpret though" is the immediate guardrail. Yahir is at the age where he's noticing institutional conformity (school, family, town) and writing the rebellion-instinct down, but already adding the "but actually following directions sometimes is fine" hedge. The hedge is what makes this Yahir-shaped rather than generic teen-rebellion.

This is the same note-shape that produces "don't do it for the money, do it for the thing that will change lives" 26 days later, and that produces "it doesn't matter what happens, it matters how you react" in early November. October-November 2019 is when manifesto aphorism mode turns on.

The line tracks with the be your own original concept that gets formalized later — the rejection of pre-scripted-life in favor of self-chosen rules.

Privacy note

None — no names, no PII.

Connections

  • 2019 10 12 bill gates books cope · 2019 10 30 do it for the thing that changes lives · 2019 11 09 it doesnt matter react — same fall-2019 aphorism cluster
  • be your own original · asking the right question
  • grants pass teen era
  • normal neutral to everything — the December-2018 sibling matrix-thinking note

related.label: Don't do it for the money, do it for the thing that will change people's lives
Hide behind Bill Gates books because you're too sad to admit you don't know anything
It doesn't matter what happens to you — react maxim (2019)
